trades.lesson: Market data was unavailable for evaluation.

lessons_learned.observation: The absence of reliable pricing data for LUCID blocked accurate tracking of whether the robotaxi thesis outperformed the NASDAQ, exposing a gap in execution
lessons_learned.adjustment: Verify data availability from multiple providers before declaring a weekly hold closed

lessons_learned.observation: The bid-ask-spread / order-flow-slippage lesson was taught in 6 of 8 recent episodes with nearly identical framing. Listeners tuned out after the third repeat.
lessons_learned.adjustment: Never re-teach bid_ask_spread or order_flow_slippage within their 45-day cooldown. If the only interesting mechanic today is a stale one, pick a different mechanic — freshness beats any single lesson.

lessons_learned.observation: The last 10 picks skewed 35% toward precious metals (Fortuna, SSR, West Red Lake) with zero flag raised on air. A real portfolio would have rebalanced.
lessons_learned.adjustment: Cap any single sector at 30% of the trailing 10-trade window. When the sector-concentration warning fires, the Practice Investment MUST pick a different sector that episode.

lessons_learned.observation: No episode has ever named the portfolio's alpha versus the NASDAQ Composite. Listeners have no way to judge whether the show is beating the market.
lessons_learned.adjustment: Every episode must state NASDAQ Composite level, portfolio YTD return, and YTD alpha in the Market Pulse segment. Fridays add the weekly alpha. Monthly episodes add month-to-date alpha.

lessons_learned.observation: Quick Hits consistently slipped into pure news reading (maple syrup shortage, Ackman's Universal Music bid, craft-brewer cost absorption) with no investing angle.
lessons_learned.adjustment: Every Quick Hit ends with an **Action:** line containing exactly one concrete portfolio decision stated as a verb phrase. Items without a concrete action are rejected at the digest stage.

lessons_learned.observation: Even a fundamental catalyst around robotaxi partnerships and bill-of-materials cost reductions cannot be properly scored when price data is missing, limiting our ability to judge whether the thesis was pricing in. This exposes a process vulnerability in data reliance that must be fixed to accurately track whether simulated picks are beating the NASDAQ
lessons_learned.adjustment: Verify data availability from multiple providers before declaring a weekly hold closed
